prevalence.trend: stable; improved diagnosis with access to genetic testing

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but high per-patient disability and cardiomyopathy burden

vulnerablePopulations: patients with cardiomyopathy or diabetes complications
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Progressive ataxia with neurologic signs plus genetic confirmation of FXN expansion; evaluate cardiomyopathy (ECG/echo) and diabetes risk

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis after symptoms and referral
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Limited (no cure); supportive care reduces complications and improves function

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnostic delays common outside tertiary neurology; genetic testing access is variable
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Physiotherapy and mobility aids, cardiology management, diabetes screening, speech therapy and psychosocial support
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rehab access and long-term multidisciplinary follow-up are uneven; cost barriers for genetics and devices
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Supportive care pathways can be implemented once diagnosis is established
